use rayon::prelude::*;
use std::fs;

// GOOD: I/O done sequentially or in dedicated async runtime, CPU work parallelized
fn process_files(paths: &[String]) -> Vec<ProcessedFile> {
    // Read files sequentially (I/O bound)
    let contents: Vec<(String, String)> = paths.iter()
        .filter_map(|path| {
            fs::read_to_string(path).ok().map(|content| (path.clone(), content))
        })
        .collect();

    // Parse in parallel (CPU bound)
    contents.par_iter()
        .map(|(path, content)| {
            let parsed = parse_content(content);
            ProcessedFile {
                path: path.clone(),
                data: parsed,
            }
        })
        .collect()
}

// GOOD: use async runtime for I/O, Rayon for CPU
async fn fetch_and_process(urls: &[String]) -> Vec<Result> {
    let bodies: Vec<String> = futures::future::join_all(
        urls.iter().map(|url| async {
            reqwest::get(url).await.unwrap().text().await.unwrap()
        })
    ).await;

    bodies.par_iter()
        .map(|body| process_body(body))
        .collect()
}
